title: Contains Duplicate
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ proposals for this challenge. The minimum-memory champion is implemented in C; see crossReferences.

variants.languageReason: std::sort is introsort with an inlined comparator: fast on any input, O(1) extra space, robust. Best practical balance.
A flat open-addressing hash set in contiguous arrays is far more cache-friendly than std::unordered_set; with early exit it wins wall-clock, especially on duplicate-heavy inputs.
variants.approachJustification: Open-addressing hash set, linear probing, Murmur3-style finalizer, returns on first collision.

reasoningSummary: Wide value range rules out counting tricks. Sorting (recommended) is O(1) extra memory and ties hashing in practice at n <= 1e5; hashing (speed) wins on early duplicates or large n.
timeComplexity: O(n log n) recommended / O(n) average (hash)
